steps.why: Retrieving Pokedex Ts Readline Behavior Tests through the concrete injected abortable readline session adapter case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use controlled readable and writable streams to prove prompts, commands, EOF, abort, error, close, and post-close behavior.

hints: Name the Pokedex Ts Readline Behavior Tests precondition or invariant that the lab case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A lab TypeScript team handles case 3221 during a shared classroom network exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove one readline interface, abortable questions, EOF handling, ownership, and idempotent close; each Promise, AbortSignal listener, timer, stream, and handle needs explicit ownership; then reorder one Promise, waiter, cache, timeout, or queue settlement and exclude stale state. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use controlled readable and writable streams to prove prompts, commands, EOF, abort, error, close, and post-close behavior.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Calling a dispatch function proves readline lifecycle.
